basics.name: Ivan Rybokolnikov
basics.label: Senior Backend & Systems Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Backend / Systems Engineer focused on high-load distributed systems, OLTP transactions, and modular monoliths. I specialize in authoritative backends (Node.js/TypeScript) where the server is the single source of truth, state mutates constantly, and data consistency is critical. Actively utilizing Golang to offload CPU-bound bottlenecks and eliminate event loop lag. Looking for complex highload challenges in a rigorous engineering culture.

work.name: Confidential Web3/Metaverse Startup
work.summary: Played a key architectural role in scaling a spatial platform from MVP to production. Architected the off-chain authoritative backend, ensuring strict state synchronization and fault-tolerant mechanics for 1,000+ peak concurrent users (CCU) per isolated instance.
highlights: Engineered real-time domain systems and highly concurrent, transactional virtual economy subsystems.
Optimized DB I/O by condensing session lifecycles into 8 atomic transactions, ensuring strict ACID compliance.
Engineered an in-memory spatial state manager using write-behind caching and event batching, aggregating high-frequency position updates to prevent PostgreSQL transaction exhaustion.
Architected a Golang microservice via NATS to offload CPU-bound calculations from the Node.js event loop, completely eliminating lag for 1,000+ active stateful sessions.
Designed complex multi-stage state machines with strict server-side transitions and a distributed RBAC session sandboxing system.
Built a reliable bi-directional event routing gateway to handle real-time data sync between web interfaces (CEF), native 3D bindings (V8), and backend services.
